# `huggingface_hub` v1.24.0 cache fixtures

These records are generated by `generate.py` against `huggingface_hub` v1.24.0
at commit `36fd32c84d630f455a23b9a3bc4dc7b76d19cdde`. `provenance.json`
also pins the Git blob identity of every upstream writer module used by the
generator.

`inventory.json` describes the Python-written standard cache below `cache/`.
The corpus covers model, dataset, and Space repositories; bare and namespaced
identifiers; a slash-containing ref; nested paths; tree metadata; `.no_exist`
markers; a snapshot-only regular file; and a copied regular file with its blob
retained. The relative-symlink entry is deliberately represented by its blob
and inventory record, not by a checked-in symlink. Pass `--runtime-symlinks` on
Unix to construct that pointer through the pinned upstream helper.
Each inventory file record pins the upstream tree blob ID, optional LFS digest
and size, physical ETag name, local content SHA-256, size, and snapshot form.

The original isolated ref, tree-cache, and `local_dir` metadata codec records
remain alongside the corpus. Text records are normalized to LF after the pinned
writers run so that the checked-in bytes are deterministic across Python hosts.
Fixture presence alone does not establish full Hub-cache compatibility.

To regenerate them, check out the pinned commit in the reference repository and
run:

```text
PYTHONPATH=<reference>/src python generate.py
```

To materialize a fresh cache for external conformance tests:

```text
PYTHONPATH=<reference>/src python generate.py --output <temporary-directory> --runtime-symlinks
```

The generator verifies the imported module root, package version, source
checkout's exact commit and tag, and Git blob IDs before writing output. It also
rejects tracked changes to the upstream `pyproject.toml` or
`src/huggingface_hub` tree. Upstream text-writer output is normalized to LF, and
portable mode removes empty directories that Git cannot preserve. Omit
`--runtime-symlinks` on Windows or when refreshing the portable checked-in
corpus.
